children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Each State that receives funding under shall develop a freight plan that provides a comprehensive plan for the immediate and long-range planning activities and investments of the State with respect to freight.section 167 of title 23
an identification of significant freight system trends, needs, and issues with respect to the State;
a description of the freight policies, strategies, and performance measures that will guide the freight-related transportation investment decisions of the State;

children.label: A
children.text: multimodal critical rural freight facilities and corridors designated within the State under ; andsection 70103 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: critical rural and urban freight corridors designated within the State under ;section 167 of title 23

a description of how the plan will improve the ability of the State to meet the national multimodal freight policy goals described in and the national highway freight program goals described in ;section 70101(b) of this titlesection 167 of title 23

a description of how innovative technologies and operational strategies, including freight intelligent transportation systems, that improve the safety and efficiency of freight movement, were considered;

in the case of roadways on which travel by heavy vehicles (including mining, agricultural, energy cargo or equipment, and timber vehicles) is projected to substantially deteriorate the condition of the roadways, a description of improvements that may be required to reduce or impede the deterioration;

an inventory of facilities with freight mobility issues, such as bottlenecks, within the State, and for those facilities that are State owned or operated, a description of the strategies the State is employing to address the freight mobility issues;
consideration of any significant congestion or delay caused by freight movements and any strategies to mitigate that congestion or delay;

a freight investment plan that, subject to subsection (c)(2), includes a list of priority projects and describes how funds made available to carry out would be invested and matched;section 167 of title 23
the most recent commercial motor vehicle parking facilities assessment conducted by the State under subsection (f);
the most recent supply chain cargo flows in the State, expressed by mode of transportation;
an inventory of commercial ports in the State;
if applicable, consideration of the findings or recommendations made by any multi-State freight compact to which the State is a party under section 70204;
the impacts of e-commerce on freight infrastructure in the State;
considerations of military freight;

children.label: A
children.text: the severity of impacts of extreme weather and natural disasters on freight mobility;

children.label: B
children.text: the impacts of freight movement on local air pollution;

children.label: C
children.text: the impacts of freight movement on flooding and stormwater runoff; and

children.label: D
children.text: the impacts of freight movement on wildlife habitat loss; and
consultation with the State freight advisory committee, if applicable.

children.heading: .—Incorporation
children.label: 1 .—Incorporation
children.text: A State freight plan described in subsection (a) may be developed separately from or incorporated into the statewide strategic long-range transportation plan required by .section 135 of title 23

children.heading: .—Fiscal constraint
children.label: 2 .—Fiscal constraint
children.text: The freight investment plan component of a freight plan shall include a project, or an identified phase of a project, only if funding for completion of the project can reasonably be anticipated to be available for the project within the time period identified in the freight investment plan.

children.heading: .—Planning Period
children.label: d .—Planning Period
children.text: A State freight plan described in subsection (a) shall address a 8-year forecast period.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A State shall update a State freight plan described in subsection (a) not less frequently than once every 4 years.

children.heading: .—Freight investment plan
children.label: 2 .—Freight investment plan
children.text: A State may update a freight investment plan described in subsection (b)(9) more frequently than is required under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Commercial Motor Vehicle Parking Facilities Assessments
children.label: f .—Commercial Motor Vehicle Parking Facilities Assessments

children.text: the capability of the State, together with the private sector in the State, to provide adequate parking facilities and rest facilities for commercial motor vehicles engaged in interstate transportation;
the volume of commercial motor vehicle traffic in the State; and

whether there exist any areas within the State with a shortage of adequate commercial motor vehicle parking facilities, including an analysis (economic or otherwise, as the State determines to be appropriate) of the underlying causes of such a shortage.
enhance reliability or redundancy of freight transportation; or
incorporate the ability to rapidly restore access and reliability with respect to freight transportation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation shall approve a State freight plan described in subsection (a) if the plan achieves compliance with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: .—Savings provision
children.label: 2 .—Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this subsection establishes new procedural requirements for the approval of a State freight plan described in subsection (a).
